title: Binary Search
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` which is sorted in ascending order, and an integer `target`, write a function to search `target` in `nums`. If `target` exists, then return its index. Otherwise, return `-1`.

You must write an algorithm with **O(log n)** runtime complexity.
constraints: nums is sorted in ascending order

hint_plan.level_1: Halve the search space each step by comparing target to the middle element.
hint_plan.level_2: Two pointers left/right or classic while lo <= hi mid pattern.
hint_plan.level_3: If nums[mid] == target return mid; elif target < nums[mid] search left half else right.
hint_plan.level_4: lo, hi = 0, len(nums)-1; while lo <= hi: mid = (lo+hi)//2; compare and shrink interval.

canonical_solution_summary: Classic binary search on sorted array with inclusive or exclusive bounds.
